ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "serial" INTEGER /* Serial number of the employee */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the employee */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the employee */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the employee */,
  "sex" INTEGER /* Sex of the employee (0: male, 1: female) */,
  "minority" INTEGER /* Minority status of the employee (0: non-minority, 1: minority) */,
  "deprived" INTEGER /* Socioeconomic status of the employee (0: non-deprived, 1: deprived) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department where the employee works */,
  "position" TEXT /* Position or job title of the employee */,
  "hire_date" TEXT /* Date when the employee was hired */,
  "termination_date" TEXT /* Date when the employee was terminated (if applicable) */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the employee is currently active */,
  "salary" REAL /* Annual salary of the employee */,
  "address" TEXT /* Residential address of the employee */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the employee */,
  "emergency_contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the emergency contact */,
  "emergency_contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the emergency contact */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "work_hours" (
  "work_hour_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each work hour record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee who worked the hours */,
  "hours_worked" REAL /* Number of hours worked */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date when the hours were worked */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the work period */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the work period */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project the employee worked on */,
  "task_description" TEXT /* Description of the task performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("work_hour_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_work_hours_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_work_hours_project_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES projects ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "wellbeing" (
  "wellbeing_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each wellbeing record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee who has the wellbeing score */,
  "wellbeing_score" INTEGER /* Wellbeing score of the employee */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date when the wellbeing score was recorded */,
  "stress_level" INTEGER /* Stress level of the employee (scale 1-10) */,
  "mental_health_score" INTEGER /* Mental health score of the employee (scale 1-100) */,
  "physical_health_score" INTEGER /* Physical health score of the employee (scale 1-100) */,
  "feedback" TEXT /* Additional feedback or comments from the employee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("wellbeing_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_wellbeing_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee whose data the user can access */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, manager, employee) */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for login */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "is_admin" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user has admin privileges */,
  "is_manager" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user has manager privileges */,
  "is_employee" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user has employee privileges */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "projects" (
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each project */,
  "project_name" TEXT /* Name of the project */,
  "project_description" TEXT /* Description of the project */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the project */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the project */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget allocated for the project */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the project (e.g., active, completed, on hold) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_id")
)
